questions.question: When should you use the Write tool instead of the Edit tool?
options: When changing a single line in an existing file
When renaming a variable throughout a file
When creating a brand-new file or doing a complete rewrite
When fixing a typo in a comment
questions.explanation: Write is for creating new files or completely overwriting existing ones. For targeted modifications, Edit is preferred because it only sends the diff.

questions.question: What happens if the Edit tool's old_string matches multiple locations in the file?
options: It replaces the first occurrence only
It replaces all occurrences automatically
The edit fails with an error
It prompts the user to choose which occurrence
questions.explanation: The Edit tool requires old_string to be unique in the file. If multiple matches exist, the edit fails. You must either add more context to make it unique or use replace_all: true.

questions.question: What is the default line limit when using the Read tool without specifying offset or limit?
options: 500 lines
1000 lines
2000 lines
Unlimited
questions.explanation: By default, the Read tool returns up to 2000 lines from the beginning of the file. Use offset and limit parameters for larger files.

questions.question: What is required when reading a PDF file longer than 10 pages?
options: Use the Bash tool with a PDF reader instead
Specify the pages parameter (max 20 pages per request)
Convert it to text first using a separate tool
Nothing special — Read handles all PDFs automatically
questions.explanation: For PDFs longer than 10 pages, the pages parameter is required (e.g., pages: '1-5'). Maximum 20 pages per request. Reading a large PDF without specifying pages will fail.

questions.question: Why must you read a file before editing it?
options: To check file permissions
To ensure the file is not locked by another process
Because Edit and Write will fail without a prior Read in the conversation
To count the total number of lines
questions.explanation: Both Edit and Write tools require that you have Read the file in the current conversation. This ensures you know the exact contents and can provide accurate old_string values for edits.

questions.question: What is the correct way to read the contents of a directory?
options: Use Read with the directory path
Use Glob with pattern '*'
Use ls via the Bash tool
Use the Edit tool in read-only mode
questions.explanation: The Read tool can only read files, not directories. To list directory contents, use the ls command via the Bash tool.
